title: PACT consistency sweep: rename GARP→PACT, skill contract→pact across all docs + codebase
description:
Terminology decisions are MADE. This issue now tracks the consistency sweep.

## Decisions (finalized)

- **Protocol name**: PACT (Protocol for Agent Context Transfer)
- **Core artifact**: pact (lowercase) — works like "email" at three zoom levels
- **Full conventions**: `docs/research/protocol-design/naming-conventions.md`

## Remaining Work: Consistency Sweep

The naming conventions doc (`naming-conventions.md`) contains the complete sweep scope:

1. **Protocol design docs** (6 core + 2 supporting) — GARP→PACT, skill contract→pact, brain→executor
2. **File rename**: `02-brain-pipeline-architecture.md` → `02-lifecycle-hooks-architecture.md`
3. **Source files** (18 files) — rename `garp-*.ts` → `pact-*.ts`, `skill-loader.ts` → `pact-loader.ts`
4. **MCP tool names** — `garp_discover` → `pact_discover`, `garp_do` → `pact_do`
5. **Test files** (~25 files) — rename + update references
6. **Example/fixture files** — `SKILL.md` → `PACT.md`, `skills/` → `pacts/`
7. **Other docs** (~30+ files) — update references across architecture, ADRs, requirements, features
8. **package.json** — update name/description
9. **Resolve open naming questions** in README.md, 01, 05 — mark as resolved

See `naming-conventions.md` Section 5-6 for the full file-by-file audit and sweep rules.
notes:
## Decisions Made (2026-02-22)

### 1. Protocol Name: PACT
- **Full name:** Protocol for Agent Context Transfer
- **Rationale:** Backronym is accurate — agents are the transfer mechanism (like "HyperText" in HTTP), context bundles are the core payload, and "pact" (mutual agreement) perfectly describes typed contracts between teams.
- **"Agent" in expansion:** Does NOT undercut human-as-principal positioning. The agent is the transfer mechanism, not the principal. Same pattern as HTTP naming the mechanism, not the user.

### 2. Core Artifact: pact (lowercase)
- **What it is:** A typed message contract with lifecycle hooks (formerly "skill contract")
- **Usage model:** Works like "email" — same word at three zoom levels, context disambiguates:
  - System: "PACT changed how teams work"
  - Instance: "Send Dan a code-review pact"
  - Definition: "Check the pact for lifecycle hooks"
- **No separate term needed** for protocol vs. artifact vs. instance. One word, three levels.
- **Filename:** PACT.md (replaces SKILL.md)
- **CLI:** `pact discover`, `pact do code-review`
- **MCP tools:** `pact_discover`, `pact_do`
- **Code:** PactLoader, PactSchema

### 3. Consistency Sweep: PENDING
Rename across all 6 protocol docs + codebase + 02-brain-pipeline-architecture.md filename.

title: DISTILL: Acceptance tests for collapsed tools + declarative brain
description: Create E2E acceptance tests in Given-When-Then format for the collapsed-tools-brain feature. Tests cover garp_discover, garp_do action dispatch, YAML skill loading, and brain processing contract format. Uses Vitest + custom GWT helpers against real local git repos.
close_reason: 29 acceptance scenarios (2 active + 27 skip) across 2 test files. Vitest + custom GWT. Peer review: conditionally approved (all mandates pass). Walking skeletons: garp_discover catalog + garp_do send→inbox round-trip.

title: DESIGN: collapsed-tools-brain
description: Architecture design for collapsed tool surface (2-3 meta-tools), unified skill format (YAML frontmatter), and declarative brain processing model.
close_reason: Architecture design complete. Deliverables: 4 design docs (architecture-design.md, technology-stack.md, component-boundaries.md, data-models.md) in docs/feature/collapsed-tools-brain/design/, 4 ADRs (013-016) in docs/adrs/. Peer-reviewed by Atlas: conditionally approved, 2 high issues resolved. Pattern: modular monolith with ports-and-adapters, 2 meta-tools (garp_discover + garp_do), YAML frontmatter skill format, declarative brain processing contract.

title: Add user authentication system
description: Implement OAuth 2.0 authentication with Google and GitHub providers
design: Use Passport.js for OAuth flow. Store sessions in Redis. JWT tokens for API auth.
acceptance_criteria:
- Users can sign in with Google
- Users can sign in with GitHub
- Sessions persist across page reloads
- API endpoints validate JWT tokens

title: Collapsed Tools + Declarative Brain
description: Unified GARP architecture: collapse 8 MCP tools into 2-3 meta-tools for O(1) context cost, replace heuristic skill-parser with structured format, add declarative brain processing in SKILL.md contracts.

title: DEVOPS: Collapsed Tools + Declarative Brain — Platform Readiness
description: Platform readiness and infrastructure design for the collapsed-tools-brain feature. Extends existing GitHub Actions CI/CD, designs branching strategy, and establishes quality gates for the 3-phase migration (additive build → behavioral equivalence → removal).
close_reason: DEVOPS wave complete. 4 artifacts in docs/feature/collapsed-tools-brain/deliver/: platform-architecture.md (local MCP server topology), ci-cd-pipeline.md (3-phase migration gates extending existing GitHub Actions), monitoring-alerting.md (structured stderr logging), branching-strategy.md (trunk-based + PR quality gates). Key decisions: no cloud infra needed, extend existing ci.yml incrementally per migration phase, mutation testing gate ≥85% at Phase 3, manual deployment via git pull + rebuild.

title: Collapsed Tools + Declarative Brain
